summary: 1967 Swedish film directed by Vilgot Sjoman, one half of a two-part erotic film ('Yellow' and 'Blue' segments) shot simultaneously as a single project titled I Am Curious. Follows a young woman conducting mock interviews about Swedish society, class, and gender politics; its cast includes a real cameo by future Swedish Prime Minister Olof Palme, and it incorporates documentary footage including a Martin Luther King Jr. interview. Best known outside quizbowl for its explicit sexual content, which triggered a landmark U.S. customs seizure and obscenity case, and for reportedly provoking a public scuffle between Jackie Kennedy and a paparazzo after she watched it.

works.name: Structure and Content
works.description: I Am Curious (Yellow) is one of two color-titled segments (with I Am Curious (Blue)) that together make up Vilgot Sjoman's I Am Curious project, shot from the same footage and released as separate features. The film's mock-documentary interview format includes a real cameo appearance by Olof Palme, later Sweden's prime minister, and incorporates genuine documentary footage of a Martin Luther King Jr. interview alongside its fictional narrative.

clues.clue: Two "Blue" and "Yellow" segments comprise this Swedish erotic film, titled I Am Curious
examples: Two "Blue" and "Yellow" segments comprise this country's erotic film I am Curious.

clues.clue: Future Swedish Prime Minister Olof Palme cameoed in this film, which includes footage of an interview with Martin Luther King Jr.
examples: It's not Canada, but a prime minister of this country had a long-term affair with Shirley Maclaine and cameoed in a film that includes footage of an interview with MLK.

works.name: Notoriety and Reception
works.description: The film's explicit content made it a flashpoint in the U.S., where it was seized by customs and became the subject of an obscenity case; its notoriety also produced a much-repeated Hollywood anecdote about Jackie Kennedy.

clues.clue: Jackie Kennedy allegedly used judo to beat up a paparazzo outside a theatre after watching this film
examples: This woman allegedly used judo to beat up a paparazzo outside a theatre after watching I am Curious (Yellow).

comprehensive_summary:
I Am Curious (Yellow) (1967) is a Swedish film directed by Vilgot Sjoman. It forms one half of a single project, I Am Curious, whose footage was split into two color-titled features, "Yellow" and "Blue" (I Am Curious (Blue) followed in 1968). The film uses a mock-documentary, interview-driven structure to follow a young woman investigating Swedish society's class divisions, politics, and gender relations. Its cast of cameos includes Olof Palme, then a rising Swedish politician who would later become prime minister, and the film incorporates real documentary footage, including an interview with Martin Luther King Jr.

In quizbowl, the film shows up almost exclusively as a passing reference within broader Sweden and pop-culture-adjacent tossups rather than as a heavily clued answerline in its own right. It is most identified by its two-segment "Blue"/"Yellow" structure and the Palme/MLK footage detail. Its explicit sexual content made it notorious in the United States, where prints were seized by customs and the ensuing legal fight became a landmark U.S. obscenity case. The film's reputation for scandal also generated a famous, oft-repeated anecdote that Jackie Kennedy scuffled with a paparazzo (reportedly using judo) outside a New York theater after seeing it.
recursive_suggestions: Vilgot Sjoman — director-level treatment would cover I Am Curious (Blue) and his other films; only a single passing cameo/context clue exists for this film, so almost all real canon is at the director level or not yet clued at all.

Olof Palme — appears only as an incidental cameo detail here; a dedicated History/current-events topic would need entirely different (assassination, premiership) clue material, not covered by this Fine Arts search.
